title: quercus × coutinhoi subsp. ferreirae
intro: Quercus × coutinhoi subsp. ferreirae is an interspecific hybrid of the Quercus robur and Quercus faginea tree species, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing cholesterol levels and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Quercus × coutinhoi subsp. ferreirae are typically made by infusing the leaves and bark in hot water to create a tea or decoction, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
